TextENGNew: Never before had I woken with such crystalline clarity.
5:59 -- one minute before my alarm was set to go off.@k ...I was amazed at my own internal clock's precision.
I'd finished all my preparations for school before going to bed.
I changed quickly and descended to the deserted lower floor.
Mom still seemed to be asleep.@k ...Neither breakfast nor a packed lunch was ready.
I'd only declared unilaterally yesterday that I'd be leaving early, so I supposed it couldn't be helped.
I laid out bread and jam, added some instant cocoa, and had just barely assembled something resembling breakfast when Mom shuffled in, still half-asleep.
``...Oh, Keiichi, you're up early today.@k@vS01/00/122700053. ......A school event or something?``
changeReason: Clarifies that bread/jam and cocoa are separate items rather than the old version's confusing 'topped [bread] with instant cocoa'

TextENGNew: ``Not really.``
I gave a curt reply, stuffed two slices of bread into my stomach, grabbed my bag, and stood up.
``You're leaving already?@k@vS01/00/122700055. What about your lunch box?@k@vS01/00/122700056. If you don't wait a bit, I can't have it ready.``
If I waited for her to make my lunch, I'd end up leaving at the same time as usual.
And that would raise the chances of running into Rena and Mion on the way...
That's right. Starting today, I was going to school alone.
``I'll manage today.``
``Then what will you do for lunch?``
``I'll slip out and buy some bread at the shop.``
``Is that so? ...Then here's some lunch money.@k@vS01/00/122700059. Make sure you bring back the receipt.``
I took the thousand-yen bill from Mom and shoved it carelessly into my pocket.
``You're leaving so early -- are Rena-chan and the others up this early too...?``
changeReason: Adds 'and the others' matching たち in the Japanese, where the old translation only mentions Rena

TextENGNew: ``No. Just me.``
``...Did you tell Rena-chan you'd be leaving early?``
......It's not like I had any obligation to report every little thing to her...?
Tired of the barrage of questions that were hard to answer one by one, I shot back an exasperated look.
``When Rena comes, just tell her I went ahead.``
``W-Wait, Keiichi?! Hold on...!``
It wasn't that I didn't trust my parents...@k But the fact that I couldn't depend on them either was also true.
If they couldn't help me... then at the very least, I needed them to stay uninvolved.@k That was safer for everyone.
I shut the door with a sharp snap, cutting off Mom's grating voice.
For the first time since moving here, I set foot on the road to school entirely alone.
Until now, I'd always walked the same route at the same time.
So I'd always met the same people at the same spots.
But today was different.
I didn't meet the people I normally would, and the people who should have been in their usual places weren't there.
Of course, Rena wasn't at our usual meeting spot, and no one was at the place where I'd normally meet Mion either.
The length of the tree shadows, the morning air, the intensity of the sun -- all of it was completely different from the mornings I knew.
It was an unmistakable sense of wrongness.
Because I'd broken the script that Hinamizawa had prepared, the cast members positioned to deceive me hadn't made it to their marks in time... I couldn't shake that impression.
``...My, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS01/00/villaA02010. You're up early today. Are you all meeting up in the morning?``
The person who called out to me was someone I always passed while they were walking along the paddy ridges.
......Their name was... uh... I'd forgotten.@k ...And of course, we weren't crossing paths in the usual spot.
``I just woke up early today... thought I'd get a change of pace.@k@vS01/01/120100399. Please don't worry about it.``
I brushed it off with a vague excuse.
``What about Rena-chan and Mion-chan?@k@vS01/00/villaA02012. Are you alone today?``
``Well... yeah...``
The same kind of questions Mom had asked.
So I gave the same bland, noncommittal answers I'd given her.
...It was irritating, being asked about Rena and the others every time I met someone.
Though I supposed it was only natural.
...For so long, we'd always been together, always friendly, always inseparable.
Even I felt that if I let my guard down, we could still be frie..................
...Stop it, Keiichi.@k Don't think any further.
You spent all of yesterday thinking about how dangerous that kind of softness was, didn't you...
HONK HONK HOOOONK!!!
A car horn, out of nowhere.
Lost in thought as I was, that horn had come from far too close.
The cold, mechanical bulk of a vehicle was bearing down on me from behind at high speed, and I'd been completely oblivious to its approach.
By the time I whipped around, the massive body of the van was right there.
I'd seen plenty of cars pull to the far shoulder to avoid a pedestrian, but this one had done the opposite.
It was as if there were someone on the opposite shoulder, and the car had swerved to this side to avoid them... That's how it felt.
That placid, peacetime train of thought delayed me from realizing something far more urgent.
That hulking mass, bearing down right in front of me.@k ...Was I about to be run over?!?!
In that instant, my skull flooded with a painfully cold liquid...!!
In that moment, the scene before me -- no, time itself -- froze solid.
In that silent, frozen moment, I compared the van -- so close that there was no way to dodge -- with my own ridiculous pose, twisted halfway around.
In this position, there was no way I could leap aside to evade...
If I lost focus for even a second, the frozen time would start moving again and I'd be sent flying in this pathetic stance.
Lean my upper body toward the paddy field beside the road.
...If I leaned far enough, maybe I'd get away with just being clipped by the side mirror...!
The instant that thought crossed my mind, the frozen time shattered under the roar of the vehicle.
The side mirror struck my shoulder, and with my body already twisted, it sent me spinning like a top...!!
KER-SPLASH!!!
Sent spiraling through the air, I crashed into the muddy paddy beside the road.
Drenched and covered head to toe in mud...!!
But among the choices available in that split second, this was surely the best outcome.
I was filthy, but considering I'd just been hit by a car, I was about as close to uninjured as one could hope.
I had enough in me to climb out of the paddy, glare at the stopped van, and scream 'You bastard!' at the top of my lungs.
Whether or not the driver saw me, the van suddenly peeled out and sped away.
``Wait, damn it!!@k@vS01/01/120100402. Isn't this... what they call a hit-and-run?!``
I couldn't help but hurl profanities.
The indignity of being plastered in mud hurt far more than any physical damage.
I slogged through the muddy paddy and hauled myself back onto the road.
``That's a crime, goddamn it...!!``
Shit!! I'll track that bastard down and press charges!!@k In a tiny village like this, one van can't be that hard to find...!!
The road I'd been on was narrow -- paddies on both sides, barely wide enough for a single car.
This wasn't a road you could barrel down at full throttle, let alone safely pass a pedestrian.
And yet, on this already narrow road, the car had veered even closer to my side as it passed...
...Even as I cursed, a dark shadow I'd been desperately trying to suppress was roiling up from deep inside me.
This wasn't just a hit-and-run... That car just now... had been trying to run me down, hadn't it...?
Thinking back... I had a feeling that for some time now, a car had been creeping slowly behind me.
Yes -- I'd sensed it ever since parting ways with that person on their walk... the whole time.
If it had wanted to pass me, it had had plenty of chances.
Normally, I would have grown suspicious and glanced behind me.
But... lost in my own thoughts, I cursed myself for failing to notice the signs.
And then... once the road narrowed and there was no one else around... the driver hit the accelerator...
If my reaction had been even a moment slower... it would have been no laughing matter.
As the adrenaline from nearly being struck subsided... I finally began to grasp just how terrifying what had happened truly was...
There was no doubt about it. That van had deliberately come to run me down...
A cold, viscous liquid seemed to seep from my brain, trickling slowly down my back...
......I fought to keep myself from spiraling into full-blown panic.
It was still possible this had been a genuine accident.
......Calm down, Keiichi!!
But don't be naive, Keiichi!!
Being that careless will get you killed next time!!
Stay vigilant at all times.
Don't show them any openings!!
If the enemy truly meant to kill me, next time they'd come with a far more certain method.
If I was caught off guard like this when that time came......!!
...I would accept this mud-caked state as the price of my own naivete.
Covered in mud but completely unscathed.@k Not even a sprain.
That alone could be called a stroke of luck amid misfortune.
This time, I began walking with true vigilance.
Not a shred of carelessness remained.
Until now, I'd suspected only Rena and the others.
...No -- by focusing my suspicion on them, I'd been trying to convince myself that no one else was an enemy.
Ooishi-san had said it, hadn't he...?
...That the whole village could be complicit...!!
Even mired this deep, was I really supposed to keep going through the motions of daily life...?
Wouldn't it be safest to just barricade myself at home...?
But... the moment I abandoned my routine, everyone around me might abandon theirs too...
That was a horrifying thought.
I recalled the old tales Ooishi-san had told me -- of Hinamizawa back when it was still called Onigafuchi.
A terrifying story of demons hunting their prey in a village-wide pursuit, surrounding them, devouring them.
One must not interfere with the demons' hunt.
changeReason: Adds 'hunt' matching 狩り in the Japanese; the old translation omits 'the demons' hunt'

TextENGNew: One must pretend not to see...
The enemy was numerous -- the entire village.@k ...The villagers, blindly devoted to the curse, would never come to my aid.
The suddenly intensified sunlight sent a wave of dizziness through me.
......I didn't know what was what anymore...
When I suspected human hands, the shadow of Oyashiro-sama's curse flickered into view...@k When I suspected the curse, human shadows emerged from hiding.
What was coincidence, and what was deliberate?
...Who was the enemy, and who was merely a bystander?
No -- more than any of that, what I truly wanted to know was...... why had I become a target in the first place?
Surely, someday... the answer would present itself in a form even I could understand...
I didn't care when that day came.
......Until then... I would absolutely, positively, not die......!
That alone was the fighting spirit keeping me alive.
I'd seen the metal bat I wanted in the gym storage shed, but a padlock on the door kept me out.
At the very least, I wanted to get my hands on it before anyone else arrived at school...
Growing somewhat anxious, I circled around the school building.
But all I could find were things like wooden beams -- nothing I could plausibly bring into the classroom.
Then... a reverse approach.@k ...I should search inside the classroom.
changeReason: Corrects 'had an epiphany' to 'a reverse approach' matching 発想の転換 (reversal of thinking)

TextENGNew: If something was already in the classroom, it wouldn't raise any questions.
I could see everyone's indoor shoes still lined up in the shoe lockers at the entrance.
Coming early had paid off. No one else was here yet.
What might be in the classroom...?
I didn't expect to find anything as effective as a bat.
But I had no other choice right now.
I needed a substitute until the gym storage was unlocked.
...Until now, I'd harbored a kind of naive faith that I wouldn't be attacked while at school.
But... that kind of soft thinking could no longer protect me.
They seemed to be encroaching, bit by bit, into parts of my life that had once been inviolable.
...Eventually... even my own house might no longer be safe...
......That was a terrifying thought.
...But looking away from the worst-case scenario seemed even more terrifying...
I would survive, no matter what.
...As long as I was alive, I could surely escape this labyrinth of absurdity.@k ......Surely.
My search of the classroom quickly hit a dead end.
Obviously.
There was no way weapons would just be lying around a classroom.
In an emergency, was my only option to swing my own chair around...?
Then my eyes fell on the row of lockers that had been repurposed for personal storage.
Among them was Mion's locker, stuffed to the brim with games she called 'club supplies.'
There was one for every student in the class, all lined up in a row.@k Mine was there too, of course.
Right -- there should still be a tracksuit in my locker.
...Being covered in mud like this would definitely look strange.@k I should change into it later...
...But first, the weapon.
Once a classmate showed up, rummaging through the lockers would be difficult.
I quickly began opening them, one by one, starting from the end.
Most contained gym clothes, personal belongings, umbrellas -- that sort of thing.
......An umbrella.
...If I found nothing better, that would have to do.
I was on the verge of giving up when I opened a locker and found exactly what I'd been hoping for.
It was unmistakably a metal bat.
Well-used and fairly dirty, but perfectly serviceable.
A musty baseball uniform also hung inside the mildew-smelling locker.
The student whose locker this was probably belonged to a youth baseball team or something.
......If so, they might ask for it back.
Just then, I heard the voices of children chattering noisily as they made their way down the hallway.
Among them, I could make out Satoko and Rika-chan.
``...Good morning.``
``My, my, my, you're here early this morning, Keiichi-san.``
I casually hid the bat I was holding behind my back.
``Wh-What is with that outfit?!@k@vS01/04/120400063. You're covered in mud!``
``Y-Yeah, well... there was a little incident.@k@vS01/01/120100405. I'm going to change right away, so bear with me.``
When I said that and started stripping, Satoko turned red just as expected.
``Ch-Changing in front of a lady is completely uncivilized!!``
``I'd say a lady who stares at someone while they're changing is the uncivilized one.@k@vS01/01/120100407. There's no changing room for boys, so deal with it.``
Feigning disgust, Satoko headed out into the hallway, still blushing.
Rika-chan, on the other hand, stood there watching me prepare to change, staring intently.
``...If Rika-chan is a lady too, then I don't think it's proper to watch someone change.``
``I'm not a lady, so it's fine.``
She deliberately put on a pouting expression, gazing up at me with upturned eyes.
``......Then starting right now, you're a lady, Rika-chan.``
``Well, if I'm a lady, then I suppose I must go.``
Rika-chan seemed satisfied at being recognized as a lady, and followed Satoko out into the hallway.
Just as I breathed a sigh of relief, Rika-chan stopped and turned back.
``...Is Keiichi going to take up baseball?``
It seemed Rika-chan had noticed the metal bat.
``Yeah... just, you know.@k@vS01/01/120100411. ...Getting out of shape.@k@vS01/01/120100412. Thought I'd start practicing my swing or something...``
``Taking care of your health is a very good thing, I think.``
For someone her age, that was a rather old-fashioned thing to say.
With just that, Rika-chan started to leave again, but stopped and turned back once more.
``Please don't lose that bat, okay?``
She seemed to have figured out I'd borrowed it from someone else's locker.
There was no name plate on the locker door.
I didn't know whose bat it was, but I'd keep borrowing it until someone complained.
It would only be for a short while -- until the gym storage was unlocked.
After quickly changing into my tracksuit, I checked the time.
Having come early, there was still plenty of time before classes started.
I headed out to the schoolyard with the bat in one hand. For swing practice, of course.
I needed to establish the fact that I always carried a bat and practiced my swing as a matter of routine.
Before I knew it, the sunlight had grown stronger.
Ignoring the classmates arriving for school, I staked out a shady spot beside the building.
I wasn't especially bookish, but I wasn't the athletic type either.
Jumping straight into swing practice might give me sore muscles.
...I should at least start with some warm-up stretches.
...To anyone watching, it must have looked quite wholesome and refreshing.@k ...The exact opposite of what I was feeling inside.
I gripped the metal bat and took a light swing.
...It was by no means light.
But that very weight meant it would be a reliable weapon when the time came.
Of course, I could only pray that moment would never arrive.
...Just having this on me might be enough to make them think twice about attacking.
I wanted to believe in that deterrent effect.
I jumped at the shrill voice that hit me from out of nowhere.
``Whoa, Keiichi-kun... is that the baseball team? The baseball team??``
It was Rena and Mion.
``I was surprised when they said you'd gone on ahead... but Kei-chan, what exactly are you doing?``
``Can't you tell by looking?@k@vS01/01/120100414. Practicing my swing -- aiming for nationals.@k@vS01/01/120100415. Or you can call it exercise if you want.``
``...Exercise? Kei-chan, are you really that out of shape?``
``Bet you didn't know. I've actually got a beer belly.@k@vS01/01/120100417. All soft and jiggly.``
``...S-Soft and jiggly......``
``......Rena. You don't need to imagine that.``
To derail Rena's inappropriate imagination, I reached over and mussed up her hair.
``Well then, good luck making it to regionals.@k@vS01/03/120300136. This old man's rooting for ya.``

``...Want me to tell you which high schools have strong baseball programs?@k@vS01/03/120300138. There's this amazing lefty pitcher named Kameda-kun at one of them.@k@vS01/03/120300139. What? You've never heard of him?``
Somehow I'd been christened a baseball nut before I knew it, but whatever.
If I actually competed, making it to nationals would be a breeze, naturally.
After all, I'd be both pitcher and catcher.
I'd throw the ball, outrun my own pitch, switch to catcher, and catch it -- assuming I had that kind of superhuman speed.
I couldn't help but laugh dryly at how absurd that image was...
Then, snapping back to reality, I slammed the bat hard against the ground.
``......Damn it!!!@k@vS01/01/120100420. What the hell am I laughing at?!``
I struck the ground over and over.@k ...With each impact, the reverberating shock stung painfully through my hands.
If they smile at me like that... I'll... I'll...!!
Don't let anyone in. Don't trust anyone. No matter how many times I told myself that... if they smiled at me like that... I'd...!!!
I knew full well that something sinister lurked behind the gentle smiles of my friends!
......But... I still couldn't bring myself to believe it.
Could something like a split personality really be real...?
Like Rena had confessed to her doctor... could they simply be possessed by Oyashiro-sama...?
In other words... did a supernatural entity called Oyashiro-sama truly exist, possessing everyone and targeting my life...?
Ahh... if only that were the case...
If everyone had always been my true friends, and everything was Oyashiro-sama's doing...
``You damned fool, Maebara Keiichi!!!!! Enough already!``
I screamed from the very bottom of my lungs with everything I had and hoisted the bat high.
``...Stop being so SOOOOOFT!!!!``
With that desperate cry, I smashed the raised metal bat into the earth again and again.
With every blow, I was beating down another piece of my own weakness...
Strike.@k Forget.
Strike.@k Don't be soft.
Strike.@k Know your enemy.
Strike.@k Like hell they'd kill me.
By the time I finally regained my composure, shoulders heaving with each breath, I heard the bell ringing...
I gasped.@k ...It was the end-of-day bell.
All the tension drained from my body, and I let out a deep breath.
The entire day had been a murky, gray expanse -- somewhere between hyper-alert and half-asleep, impossible to pin down as either.
I couldn't call it comfortable, but there was a measure of relief in knowing that the sacred routine of school had not been violated.
How long would I have to keep living like this...?
This endless torment with no light at the end of the tunnel was wearing me down, bit by bit.
``Keiichi-kun!@k@vS01/02/120200204. Come on, come on!@k@vS01/02/120200205. It's time for club activities! It is!``
Rena's voice jolted me back to reality.
``Hey hey, Kei-chan, stop spacing out!@k@vS01/03/120300141. Push your desks together, come on!``
Everyone was arranging their desks face-to-face, same as always.
That's right -- it was time for their fun, fun club activities.
But I had no intention of joining in.
I began haphazardly shoving the contents of my desk into my bag, packing up to leave.
It was my own passive gesture, since I couldn't bring myself to actually say 'I'm heading home early.'
``......What's this, Kei-chan?@k@vS01/03/120300143. Going straight home again today...?``
Mion's tone was openly displeased.
``...I'm just not up for it. Could you leave me alone for a while?``
The words that left my mouth carried a displeasure that rivaled Mion's own.
The air in the room seemed to dry out.
Satoko looked like she was about to say something, but perhaps put off by the atmosphere, she swallowed her words and fell silent.
No one said a thing.@k ...Which should have meant I was free to leave.
But the gazes of all four of them pinned me in place, like the specimen pins holding down insects in a display case.
Rena was the one who broke that dry silence.
``............Keiichi-kun... you just didn't like... playing games with girls after all, did you... Did you.``
It was a tone that sent a pang of pain through my chest.
If this pain could kill me, then I wanted it to kill the soft part of me that felt it.
I clawed at my chest, violently ripping out those specimen pins that held me fast.
``That's not it at all...!``
Anything more I said would only wound me further, so I swallowed the rest.
I cut the conversation short, turned on my heel, and walked out of the classroom.
Not a single word was called after me.
The walk home was long and tedious, but I never let my guard down.
The handle of the metal bat, clenched tightly in my grip, was already slick with sweat.
Noticing this, I wiped it down with my sleeve -- I couldn't afford to have it slip in a critical moment.
Since this morning's incident, I'd become acutely sensitive to the presence of cars.
Even as I walked, I strained my ears, scanning for any ominous sounds or approaching presences...
And that was exactly why I noticed it.
Footsteps. Unmistakable.
Those footsteps had been matching mine perfectly for some time now.
From the presence I sensed... it was a single person.@k ...But I had no intention of letting my guard down.
Were they tailing me, like this morning's car, waiting until they reached a convenient spot to strike...?
If so, continuing to walk like this was unwise.
I stopped and looked back.
The tree-lined forest path was perfectly still, as though no one had been there from the start...
But I wouldn't be fooled. There had definitely been footsteps following me.
And the moment I stopped, those footsteps had stopped too.
...In other words, whoever was following me wanted to maintain their distance.
That was unmistakable proof that I was their focus.
I held my breath, waiting for the presence to grow impatient and start moving...
The trees rustled in a gentle breeze.
The higurashi cicadas joined in a grating chorus, trying to break my concentration.
Had five minutes passed?@k Or had I been standing here for thirty...?
......I was the one who broke first, nearly suffocating from holding my breath.
There was no question -- someone was lurking in those shadows, holding their breath, watching me.
...Fine then... I'd make the first move...!
I adjusted my grip on the bat.@k ...I hoisted it to my shoulder, ready to swing at a moment's notice.
``...Hey!!!@k@vS01/01/120100426. I know you're there!!``
With everything I had, I shouted at whoever was trying to wait me out in the shadows.
But... the presence in the shadows didn't budge.
Did they intend to keep their identity hidden until the very moment I found them...?
``I already know!!!@k@vS01/01/120100428. I know you're in there!!!!``
I shouted again, but still they didn't move.
Fine, then I'd go to them!!
Cautiously on guard, I closed the distance step by step...
Circling wide around the tree's shadow... I saw a figure.
The figure... was curled up small, like a frightened animal.
...When Rena realized I'd found her, a look of resignation crossed her face.
changeReason: Corrects 'her expression softened' to 'a look of resignation crossed her face' matching 観念した (resigned/given up)

TextENGNew: She looked apologetic, but wouldn't open her mouth to speak.
``......What do you want from me...!``
Refusing to accept her silence, I immediately shouted at her.
``N-Nothing really... it's just... um...``
Rena was flustered, tears welling in her eyes.@k But it was obvious she'd been following me.
``What about club?``
``......Si... Since Keiichi-kun skipped... Rena also skipped......``
``That has nothing to do with it!@k@vS01/01/120100433. Don't worry about me and just go play!``
``...B-...But... I was just worried... about you...``
Given how I'd been acting lately, it wasn't surprising she'd think something was off.
So Rena was just concerned about me... at first glance, that seemed like a natural explanation.
......But I was careful not to let my guard down so easily.
If that were truly the case, she wouldn't have needed to follow me like a shadow.
...She should have just called out to me as I was leaving and walked alongside me openly.
But Rena hadn't done that.
She'd maintained a constant distance, matching my pace exactly.@k On top of that, she'd even synchronized her footsteps with mine, skillfully masking her presence.
And when she sensed that I'd noticed her, she held her breath and tried to hide, hoping to slip by undetected.
She was wearing a timid expression, as though begging for mercy, but what Rena had been doing was unmistakably... tailing me.
``Stop following me.``
Still glaring at Rena, I slowly resumed walking.
After a short distance, she ignored my warning and started walking again, so I shouted at her once more.
``I told you to stop following me!!!``
``Eek!! ......B-But! ...Rena's house is in the same direction...!``
``Then walk ahead of me!! I'll start walking again once you're out of sight.``
I stepped aside and swung the bat aggressively, gesturing for Rena to go ahead.
``I... want to walk home... together with Keiichi-kun......``
With a pitiful expression, Rena whimpered in a voice that seemed to know exactly where my heart was most vulnerable.
......That infuriated me beyond measure.
Because it was a lie.
If she'd wanted to walk home together, she should have just called out to me.
Making up excuses like that at this point...!!
``I-I'm s-s-s-sorry, I'm sorry...!!!``
It seemed my seething fury was written plainly across my face.
......Even without me saying a word, Rena seemed to understand exactly what I was feeling.
``If you understand, then go! Move!``
I swung the bat, once again urging her to walk ahead.
Rena looked back and forth between me and the bat several times, started to walk hesitantly, then stopped again.
``...Hurry up and go. Hurry!!``
``I-I'm going!@k@vS01/02/120200215. So please stop with the bat...!!@k@vS01/02/120200216. I'm... scared...!!``
Shielding herself with her arms, Rena pointed alternately at me and the bat.
She may have realized this bat wasn't intended for baseball...
I lowered the bat, but stayed on guard as I cautiously made room for her to pass.
``Go.@k@vS01/01/120100440. ...No problem now, is there?``
Rena seemed to have no grounds left to resist.
...She slipped past me timidly, careful not to set me off.
I watched her retreating figure, but she stopped again after barely taking a few steps.
``Hey, don't stop...!``
At that moment, an especially strong gust of wind blew through, pelting my face with stinging grains of sand.
Grit flew into my eyes, turning my vision into a blurry mess of tears...!
I rubbed my eyes with my left hand while blindly swinging the bat with my right to cover the opening.
But Rena didn't use that moment of vulnerability to turn around and attack.
...No -- attack? She hadn't moved at all.
The only sign of the passing wind was her skirt, still fluttering in its wake.
The ripple in her skirt settled, and silence returned...
At that moment, another part of me issued a sharp warning of danger.
......I froze.
...Had the smell of the air... changed...?
Without my noticing, the world had filled with a strange atmosphere, as if anything -- any catastrophe -- could happen at any moment.
The air felt as though it were made of invisible concrete, entombing both Rena and me in this space.
Rena didn't move a muscle.@k ......Neither could I, staring fixedly at her back.
Rena was the first to break the silence.
I braced myself instantly.
......Because the Rena before me seemed to have transformed into someone else entirely -- someone who only looked like Rena.
changeReason: Stylistic rephrasing; both old and new correctly convey the idea of Rena seemingly transforming into a different person who merely resembles her.

TextENGNew: But... the voice was one I knew well -- bewildered and pitiable.
Shamefully, I felt relief at that pitiful voice...
``......U-Um, I'm sorry about this!@k@vS01/02/120200219. ......But, can I ask you something?!``
Too hesitant to even turn around, Rena forced her voice out with desperate effort.@k ...Trembling as she spoke.
``Uh... Um, so!@k@vS01/02/120200221. Wh-Why are you carrying a bat, I wonder. ...I wonder!``
...Rena's question wasn't unreasonable.
``I can carry whatever I want.``
``But, but... you didn't have one until yesterday...!@k@vS01/02/120200223. ......Wh-Why all of a sudden...!``
``What's wrong with doing something suddenly?@k@vS01/01/120100445. ...Is it that weird for me to have a bat?``
``B-Because, Keiichi-kun... you were never the kind of person who played baseball, were you?!@k@vS01/02/120200225. It's strange...!``
I couldn't quite grasp what answer Rena was looking for, and explaining in detail was a hassle.
``...I just suddenly felt like playing baseball.@k@vS01/01/120100447. Is there something wrong with that?``
``It's strange...!``
The instant reply irked me a little.
``I suddenly felt like playing baseball.@k@vS01/01/120100449. I wanted to practice my swing, so I'm carrying a bat.@k@vS01/01/120100450. What's weird about that?``
``Weird.@k@vS01/02/120200228. Strange.@k@vS01/02/120200229. Definitely.@k@vS01/02/120200230. Why is Keiichi-kun doing the same thing...``
``You're really getting on my nerves...@k@vS01/01/120100452. Is it really that odd for me to take up a sport?!``
I injected some menace into my voice, trying to force an end to the conversation.
As long as my suspicions about Rena remained, I had no obligation to explain myself.
``......I-I'm sorry, I'm sorry...!@k@vS01/02/120200232. Please don't be so angry...!!``
Still without turning around, Rena trembled as she rattled off apology after apology.
``J-Just one last thing!!@k@vS01/02/120200234. Just tell me one last thing...!!``
``...I'm not in the mood to talk or explain anything more.@k@vS01/01/120100454. Hurry up and go!!``
When I roared at her, Rena flinched violently, as though struck by lightning.
...Seeing her in such a pathetic state sent a stinging ache through my chest...
But despite her fear, Rena stubbornly held her ground.
Just as I was about to shout at her one more time, Rena asked her final question.
``Wh-Why is even the bat the same?!?!``
The bat was the same as what?@k I couldn't follow Rena's point.
``......What are you talking about...?``
``I mean...! Why is even the bat the same?!``
``I have absolutely no idea what you mean!@k@vS01/01/120100457. Say it clearly!!!``
Still Rena wouldn't turn around.@k She swallowed hard, then screamed.
``I mean......why is even the bat the same as Satoshi-kun's?!?!``
``Satoshi... you mean...... Huh...?``
The unexpected name left me stunned for a moment.
By 'Satoshi'... she meant the student who transferred last year?@k ......No, that wasn't right.
Rena had covered it up by saying he'd transferred, but Ooishi-san had stated clearly that he'd disappeared.
He was the student who'd sat at my desk until last year -- the one believed to have been 'spirited away' in last year's Oyashiro-sama curse.
I knew nothing about the details leading up to his disappearance.
The aunt he'd lived with was killed on the night of the Watanagashi by someone taking advantage of the curse, and shortly after, he simply vanished without a trace......
changeReason: Both say aunt killed on Watanagashi night, Satoshi vanished after; 'drug addict' vs 'someone taking advantage of the curse' vs JPN '異常者' (disturbed person) -- all approximate translations of the same concept.

TextENGNew: And this Satoshi and I were... what?
I shifted my gaze down to the bat in my hands.
......Could it be...?
'Satoshi Houjou'
It was a bit hard to make out, but written on the white tape wrapped around the bat's handle were unmistakably those words.
......So... this was Satoshi's bat...
``...Oh... I see.@k@vS01/01/120100460. So this was Satoshi's bat.@k@vS01/01/120100461. ...Nobody was using it, so I just borrowed it.``
``Th-...That's not the point!!!``
The way Rena said it... carried the ring of something sacred, something that must not be touched.
Like an offering on a household shrine... or the personal effects of the dead...
Unable to respond, I could only stand there bewildered. Without waiting for me, Rena continued.
``...Why is it... why is everything... exactly the same as Satoshi-kun?!``
What Rena was getting at went far beyond the bat belonging to Satoshi.
``...Satoshi-kun was the same!!@k@vS01/02/120200241. He was on a baseball team, sure... but he was never someone who actually liked baseball......!``
``What does that have to do with me...?``
``Satoshi-kun also... one day, out of nowhere, started carrying a bat around!!!!@k@vS01/02/120200243. He was just on a team -- he never actually played sports... and yet......!!``
So what...!@k I was about to say it, but stopped myself.
...Listen carefully, Keiichi!
Rena was trying to tell me something important...
``Satoshi-kun, you know... one day, out of the blue, started going to school alone.@k@vS01/02/120200245. Just like Keiichi-kun!!!``
``And then, one day, out of the blue, he started practicing his swing.@k@vS01/02/120200247. Just like Keiichi-kun!!!``
``And then, one day, out of the blue, he started carrying a bat everywhere.@k@vS01/02/120200249. Just like Keiichi-kun!!!!``
``And then, one day, out of the blue...............``
......And then, one day, ...what?!
There, Rena swallowed her words.
Rena's silence brought the hush flooding back.
...And at last, the full picture came into focus.
She was saying that my sequence of actions... was identical to Satoshi's.
What... did that mean?!
Until this very moment, I'd completely forgotten about Satoshi.
I'd never consciously thought about him at all.
In fact, I didn't even know what Satoshi had done.
The actions I'd taken today were supposed to be my own -- the product of careful deliberation.
......And yet... they were exactly the same as Satoshi's...???
Then Satoshi was... no, more importantly...!!
If Satoshi and I had followed the same path up to this point, the chances of what came next being the same were extremely high...??
``Rena... that day... what happened to Satoshi?!``
Rena knew...@k She knew what had become of Satoshi.@k No -- forget about some guy from the past!
Rena... knew what was going to happen... to me!!!
``A-Answer me, Rena!!@k@vS01/01/120100466. Satoshi... what happened to him?!``
Saying that, I roughly grabbed Rena's shoulder and forced her to face me.
The instant I wrenched Rena around, an electric shock surged through my entire body...!!
``I already told you.@k@vS01/02/120200252. Keiichi-kun.``
It was someone I didn't know.
At the very least, it was absolutely not the Rena Ryuugu I'd been talking to just moments ago.
Gone were every trace of the trembling and the raw emotion her voice had carried until now.
...I regretted turning her around so carelessly with an intensity I'd never felt before...!
That gaze, cold and piercing like a needle... and those lips, curled into a smile as sharp as a razor's edge......
My spine froze solid. Even my thoughts began to crystallize behind a layer of frost.
Transfixed by both of Rena's eyes, I couldn't even avert my gaze.
...As if to resurrect the terror of that time, Rena brought her face close enough that I could feel her breath.
...My entire field of vision was filled with Rena.
......And then... her already sharp lips curved further, slicing into a crescent moon arc... and she smiled.
``I already told you.@k@vS01/02/120200254. Keiichi-kun.``
After two breaths' pause, Rena repeated the same words once more.
``Satoshi-kun, you see... 'transferred.'``
changeReason: Adds quotation marks around 'transferred' emphasizing Rena's ominous, euphemistic use of the word, matching the sinister tone of the Japanese

TextENGNew: Transferred... what was that supposed to mean...?
......In my vocabulary, the word 'transfer' didn't carry the meaning Rena seemed to give it...
My throat and lips had gone bone-dry. I couldn't even manage a reply.
All I could do was swallow... my own saliva...
...To Rena, it seemed to have looked like a nod.
She released me from that gaze, stepping back two or three quiet paces.
The instant she did, my knees buckled and I crumpled gracelessly to the ground.
......Rena with her emotionless smile, and me kneeling before her in a daze......
It must have been a bizarre sight...
Rena neither mocked my pathetic state nor offered me a hand.
She simply pierced me with that quiet, steady gaze -- allowing me neither to stand nor to flee.
The metal bat was undeniably in my hand.
But... to me, a frog paralyzed under a serpent's stare, it was utterly useless.
changeReason: Corrects the metaphor from 'fly caught in her web' to 'frog paralyzed under a serpent's stare' matching the Japanese idiom 蛇に睨まれた蛙

TextENGNew: Cold sweat coated my entire body; I could feel it dripping, drop by drop...
``...You won't, will you?@k@vS01/02/120200257. Keiichi-kun.``
...At last, Rena released me from what felt like an eternity trapped in a cage of time.
But her question lacked a subject -- it was deeply abstract.
I swallowed hard once more, urging her to continue.
``......Wh-...what won't I do...?``
What was it... that she didn't want me to do...??
``'Transfer.'``
changeReason: Adds quotation marks around 'Transfer' emphasizing the ominous, euphemistic meaning
